sprint: Gate Sequence Becomes the Opening Beat -- Scene 1's Fixed Pause Removed
task: Direct, immediate follow-up on the just-shipped "Lumo Guards the Gate" rework: "use the gate sequence for the point go. we dont need any sky squence." Scene 1 ("The Sky") used to be a fixed 2400ms atmosphere-only pause -- nothing but the ambient sky decorations (clouds/particles/rays/haze) -- before the Gate, Story Egg, and Lumo ever appeared in Scene 2. That fixed SCENE1_MS delay is removed outright: the Promise.all([resolveLumo(),resolveEgg()]) that resolves the registry and mounts the Gate/Egg/Lumo now runs immediately when begin() starts, instead of waiting behind an artificial setTimeout first. The ambient sky decorations are unchanged and still form the backdrop everything sits in front of -- they were never a distinct 'scene' with mountable content of their own, so nothing about them needed to move; only the empty pause that used to precede the Gate's own appearance is gone. A real second video the user separately pushed to the repo (assets/video/gateway/gate-sequence-01.mp4, showing Lumo flying into the gate's own light) was investigated (extracted frames confirm what it shows) but deliberately NOT wired into anything yet, since it appears to depict Lumo entering the Gate -- a real conflict with the frozen canon ("Lumo never enters the Hall") that needs the user's own explicit direction before any code treats it as settled, not a decision to make unilaterally. Verified via a new Playwright timing check (the Gate wrap now appears in ~250ms instead of the old fixed ~2400ms) plus a real screenshot confirming the Gate/Lumo/greeting are all visible together on the very first meaningful frame, and a full re-run of every existing Gateway regression suite from the prior sprint (21 Traveller-path assertions, 7 Returning-Creator-path assertions, the tap-to-skip sanity check) -- all passing unchanged after one intentional fix to a test assertion that had raced against the now-instant mount timing (checking for a real video[src] early rather than trying to catch a since-narrower transient CSS class window). Both goldenBuild.js suites (World Builder v1 and v2, unaffected -- this sprint touches zero Builder files) pass unchanged. No Theme Contract/Engine V2 Scene Model/Builder/Creator Governing Rules change; confined to js/gatewaySequence.js only -- no CSS changes this time.
